topic: gym routine — trying to bulk up

turns.content: whats a good workout split for someone tryna put on some size I feel like I been goin to the gym for months and not really gettin anywhere

If you're trying to bulk up and not seeing results, the two most common issues are programming and nutrition. What does your current split look like -- how many days a week are you going and how are you splitting up body parts?

ive been doin like a bro split I guess monday chest tuesday back wednesday legs and then I do arms on friday but honestly I skip legs a lot lol and I been goin kinda random some weeks ill only make it 3 times

Yeah, that's probably a big part of the issue. A traditional bro split only hits each muscle once a week, and if you're skipping days on top of that, some muscle groups are barely getting trained. For size, you'd be better off with a push/pull/legs split or an upper/lower split -- that way you're hitting everything at least twice a week, which is better for growth.
ok so whats a push pull legs look like cuz ive heard people talk about it but ive never actually done it

It's pretty simple:

- **Push day**: Chest, shoulders, triceps (bench press, overhead press, dips, lateral raises, tricep pushdowns)
- **Pull day**: Back, biceps, rear delts (deadlifts or rows, pull-ups, curls, face pulls)
- **Legs**: Quads, hamstrings, glutes, calves (squats, leg press, Romanian deadlifts, calf raises)

You run it twice through the week -- so Push, Pull, Legs, Push, Pull, Legs, rest. That's 6 days. Or if you can only do 4-5 days, just cycle through and pick up where you left off.
6 days is a lot bro I work on job sites all day so im already pretty beat by the time I get home. could I do like 4 days and still get results

Absolutely. If you're doing physical work all day, 4 days is totally fine. I'd actually recommend an upper/lower split for 4 days:

- Monday: Upper
- Tuesday: Lower
- Wednesday: Off
- Thursday: Upper
- Friday: Lower
- Weekend: Off

You hit everything twice a week but you get more rest days. With a physical job, recovery is huge -- you won't grow if you're running yourself into the ground.
bet that sounds way more doable. what about food cuz I feel like I eat a lot but maybe im not eatin the right stuff. like I just smash whatever is around and have a protein shake after the gym

If you're not gaining weight, you're probably not eating as much as you think -- that's the number one issue for guys trying to bulk. A good starting target is roughly 1 gram of protein per pound of bodyweight per day, and then make sure your total calories are in a surplus. How much do you weigh right now?
like 175 ish so youre sayin I need 175 grams of protein a day thats insane dude thats so much chicken

Ha, it doesn't all have to be chicken. You can spread it across meals -- eggs at breakfast, a couple scoops of protein powder, ground beef or chicken for lunch and dinner, Greek yogurt as a snack. If you're getting 30-40 grams per meal across 4-5 meals and snacks, you'll hit 175 without it feeling crazy. The protein shake you're already having is a good start -- what kind are you using?
just the gold standard whey from amazon whatever the big tub is I do 2 scoops with milk after the gym. I could probably add one in the morning too before work that would help

Gold Standard is solid, can't go wrong with that. Two scoops with milk is already around 50 grams of protein right there, so adding a morning shake would put you close to 100 from shakes alone. Fill in the rest with real food at lunch and dinner and you're golden. The morning shake before a physical job is actually a really smart move -- gives you fuel and protein right when you need it.
alright sick im gonna try the upper lower thing and actually be consistent this time and eat more. appreciate it bro

Sounds like a plan. Consistency is the biggest thing -- just show up 4 times a week, eat enough protein, and the results will come. Give it like 6-8 weeks of being locked in before you judge anything. Good luck with it man.
